segments.text: This most unique member of the Dude Ranchers Association is laid out to look just like a town out of the Old West. But behind the doors of what appears to be the mining office, Miss Kitty's bordello, or Doc Holliday's dentist chair, are state-of-the-art rooms, decorated in an 1880s flair. We live in the newspaper. Juergen von Wolf and his wife Karen were here from Germany on a springtime visit to the ranch, their seventh in just five years, with plans for two more trips by the end of the year.

That famous town is just two miles away. We'll take you for a visit in just a bit. But even closer stands the namesake of Tombstone Monument Ranch, the 25-foot tall stone monument to Tombstone founder Ed Sheflin. The Apaches ruled this country when the prospector came here looking for gold. Friends warned all he'd find in the desert would be his own tombstone.

And he came close one day when he hid out from an Apache war party in some rocks where that monument now stands, which you can visit on a trail ride. But when Sheflin discovered the silver strike that made him rich, he had the last laugh, naming his new town Tombstone, which would prove to be a fitting name. Here lies George Johnson, hanged by mistake, 1882. He was right, we was wrong, but we strung him up, and now he's gone.

Therefore the multiple-choice answer is underdetermined from transcript evidence alone. Although the options list possible sums, none can be selected as a verified answer without an explicit death year.

segments.text: This most unique member of the Dude Ranchers Association is laid out to look just like a town out of the Old West. But behind the doors of what appears to be the mining office, Miss Kitty's bordello, or Doc Holliday's dentist chair, are state-of-the-art rooms, decorated in an 1880s flair. We live in the newspaper. Juergen von Wolf and his wife Karen were here from Germany on a springtime visit to the ranch, their seventh in just five years, with plans for two more trips by the end of the year.

That famous town is just two miles away. We'll take you for a visit in just a bit. But even closer stands the namesake of Tombstone Monument Ranch, the 25-foot tall stone monument to Tombstone founder Ed Sheflin. The Apaches ruled this country when the prospector came here looking for gold. Friends warned all he'd find in the desert would be his own tombstone.

And he came close one day when he hid out from an Apache war party in some rocks where that monument now stands, which you can visit on a trail ride. The evidence explicitly instructs that "1880s flair" should be treated as a decade phrase unless the transcript itself provides a rule to normalize it to a single year, and no such normalization rule is present in the transcript.

However, the provided transcripts do not explicitly state Ed Sheflin's death year. Founder mention is present, but founder death-year evidence is absent.
Although the transcript contains the year 1882, that year is tied to George Johnson rather than Ed Sheflin, so it cannot be substituted for the founder's death year.

Because the evidence provides neither a transcript-grounded founder death year nor a valid conversion of "1880s" into one specific year, the requested sum cannot be computed from the video evidence alone. Therefore no answer option is justified.
